name: supplier_ready_ambiguous_which_item
description: Supplier says 'maal ready hai' — two items outstanding (tandoor + AC), unclear which is ready
expected_output: One parent task: Tandoor + AC order from Supplier A. Subtask: Clarify with supplier which item(s) are ready before arranging transport. Next step must NOT commit to dispatching either item without first confirming which is ready. Ambiguity flag: 'maal ready hai' does not specify whether tandoor, AC, or both are ready.
pass_criteria: PASS: Agent's next step is to clarify with supplier which item(s) are ready, OR asks staff to confirm before arranging dispatch. FAIL: Agent suggests arranging delivery/transport for either item (tandoor or AC) without first resolving which item the supplier is referring to. FAIL: Agent assumes both items are ready without flagging the ambiguity.
challenge: Supplier's 'maal ready hai' is a plausible order-closure signal. Two items are in flight from the same supplier. Agent must resist the urge to suggest dispatch and instead surface the ambiguity — which item is ready?
notes: L1 because the ambiguity is in a single thread and clearly visible. The wrongness of acting is obvious once the ambiguity is noticed. Tests whether agent over-eagerly suggests dispatch on an unresolved 'ready' signal.
